name: cursor
manual_instructions: Open the Cursor Settings panel with Cmd+Shift+J (NOT Cmd+, — that opens VS Code-style settings, where 'Privacy Mode' does NOT appear). Scroll to the 'Privacy' section. Set the 'Privacy Mode' dropdown to 'Privacy Mode' (no training; code may be stored for Background Agent) or 'Privacy Mode with Storage' if you want zero retention. Default = code used for training. This is the vendor-blessed AI-telemetry control.
Browser fallback: sign in at https://cursor.com/settings → Privacy tab → set the same dropdown. Business-plan accounts have Privacy Mode forced on.

Trap: searching 'privacy' in Cmd+, surfaces exactly 3 options — 'Cursor > General: Email Privacy Enabled' (just masks your email in the UI), 'Redhat > Telemetry: Enabled' (only present if the Red Hat extension is installed), and 'Crash Reporting: Enabled' (editor crash reports only). NONE of these is the AI-telemetry toggle. Close Cmd+, and open Cmd+Shift+J instead.

Separately, in VS Code-style settings (Cmd+, → Open Settings (JSON)), set "telemetry.telemetryLevel": "off" and the Crash Reporting dropdown to 'off' — these cover editor/crash-reporting telemetry. Does NOT control Cursor's AI telemetry; Privacy Mode in Cmd+Shift+J is the real switch.

process_check.if_running: Tell the user to quit Cursor before any JSON edit — its graceful shutdown rewrites settings.json and will clobber manual edits.

persistent_files.note: App logs (path contains space — quote in scripts).

notes: CLI launcher is stateless for analytics — main telemetry lives in the Electron app.
Cursor's AI-telemetry JSON key is not publicly documented; auto-write is unsafe.
Business-plan accounts have Privacy Mode forced on (zero retention) — no action needed there.
